Fig.-4: Cyanopyridine and 4-thiazolidinone derivatives of benzimidazole
Different 2-phenyl benzimidazole derivatives have been described as powerful antibacterial with fair to
moderate efficacy by a group of researchers (Turkey), against Candida albicans and certain bacterial
types. Substituents with a 2-phenyl ring at meta and parahavebeen generated, Compounds 8 (R = NH )
2
and 9, which have antibacterial activity comparable to that of ampicillin and against Pseudomonas
aeruginosa.34, 35 Structural constraints around the benzimidazole nucleus requires for antimicrobial action
is described in Fig.-5.
Antibacterial Activity of Benzimidazole and Other Heterocyclic Hybrid Derivatives:
Coumarin–benzimidazole Hybrids as Potent Antibacterial Agents
The antibacterial potential of eleven proposed novel conjugates has been evaluated against a panel of the
pathogenic bacterial strain. Amongst all, hybrids 10 and 11 (Fig.-6) efficiently showed bactericidal
potential against Bacillus subtilis with MIC=0.95 and 6.25μg/mL, correspondingly, while standard drug
Ampicillin had MIC=25μg/mL. Compounds 11, 12, 13, and 14 showed substantial suppression of Proteus
vulgaris (ATCC 29905) at MIC 1.56, 3.12, 1.56, and 1.56μg/mL, in that order, compounds having a
halogen atom have antibacterial activity, according to molecular structure studies. Compound 10, -Cl
atom (at para position) was discovered to have the most wide-ranging- antibacterial activity. Surprisingly,
compounds having a -Cl atom (Meta or Ortho position) (14) have no bactericidal action.36
Antibacterial Potential of Novel Benzimidazole–Oxadiazole Hybrid Compounds
Antibacterial activity of 20, new benzimidazole–oxadiazole hybrid molecules was recorded against tested
bacterial strains, and MICs were compared with ciprofloxacin. Among all, compounds 15 and 16 (Fig.-7)
demonstrated the most potent antibacterial agents.

Compound 15, which had an oxadiazole nucleus with a p-chlorophenyl group at C3, and a 2-chloro-6-
fluorophenyl at C2 of the benzimidazole ring, with MIC (1.6 µg/mL against E. faecalis,3.125 µg/mL
against S. aureus, 6.25µg/mL). Compound 16, which had a 3-chloro-2-fluorophenyl instead of the 2-
chloro group (compound 15) has gram-positive activity (MIC = 1.6µg/mL).Findings indicate that the
superior activity could be recognized due to the existence of halogen atoms; chiefly Fluorine atom could
convey potentiality.37
Antibacterial activity of Benzimidazole-Pyrazole and Pyridine hybrids
The pyridine and pyrazole moiety which can block a series of enzymes and receptors is generally used in
pharmaceutical research.38To sustain their pyrimidine pools, bacteria rely on de novo pyrimidine
1895
ANTIBACTERIAL POTENTIAL OF BENZIMIDAZOLE Suvaiv et al.

Vol. 16 | No. 3 |1892-1905| July - September | 2023
generation, pyrimidine derivatives are crucial in a variety of biological processes.39,40 Conjugation of
benzimidazole with pyridine/pyrazole could serve as a lead compound with noteworthy antibacterial
potency. Pyridine and pyrazole are also multipurpose and widely investigated scaffolds for the
identification of new antibacterial agents.14 Novel benzimidazole-pyrazole and pyridine nucleus
containing hybrid molecules (Fig.-8) have been synthesized and their antibacterial activity was evaluated,
amongst all Compounds17d, 17f, 17h, and 17g were investigated most potent with MIC of 25µg/mL in
opposition to selected bacterial strains. Substitution of the hydrogen atom by the hydroxyl group results
17d which has improved activity against P. aeruginosa.41

Antibacterial Activities of Novel Benzothiazoles, Benzoxazoles, and Benzimidazoles
Molecules containing benzimidazole, benzoxazole, and benzothiazole nucleus in their structure have a
broad assortment of biological features such as antiviral42, anticancer43, and antimicrobial44 actions have
been demonstrated for these heterocycles. Pyrazoles are used as essential intermediates, and important
precursors in the synthesis of benzofuran/coumarin systems and benzimidazole, benzoxazole, and
benzothiazole derivatives respectively, although antibacterial properties of different pyrazole derivatives
have been described in the literature.45 Many bacteria have been reported to be inhibited by pyrazoles and
substituted pyrazoles.46A group of researchers evaluated the in vitro antibacterial potential (serial dilution
method) of 12 newly synthesized benzimidazoles, benzothiazoles, and benzoxazoles derivatives (Fig.-9).
The antibacterial activity of derivatives 18a, 18c, 18d, 18g, 18j, and 18k was good. 18b, 18f, and
18i demonstrated weak to moderate activity as evidenced by their MIC values. 18e and 18h were shown
to be the least potential against both tested bacterial strains, among these compounds. The new 2-

[substituted-1H-pyrazol- 4-yl] has a structure–activity connection. In contrast to the benzothiazole
moiety (18e and 18f) in target compounds, novel compounds comprising benzimidazole (18b, 18c, 18g,
18h, 18i, 18j, and 18k) and benzoxazole (18a and 18d) demonstrated broad-spectrum antibacterial profiles
against tested species. No effect of electron-donating and withdrawing groups in target compounds (18a–
18k) is observed on the bactericidal action against the tested bacterial strain.47

Novel 1, 3, 5-trisubstituted Pyrazoline Hybridized with Benzimidazole Analoguesas Antibacterial
Agents
The pyrazoline motif has to be found in a variety of physiologically active compounds. Antimicrobial48,49
anticancer50,51 anti-inflammatory52,53 and monoamine oxidase inhibitory54,55 activities were demonstrated
in some members of this class. A new series of antibacterial drugs known as thiazolyl-pyrazoline56 has
just been discovered. Hybridization of pyrazoline and N-benzyl benzimidazole fragments may serve as a
springboard for the discovery of novel antibacterial agents.15 New 1,3,5-trisubstituted pyrazoline analogs
hybridized with benzimidazole derivatives were developed (Fig.-10) and recorded for bactericidal
potential against selected pathogenic strains. Compound 28 (minimum inhibitory concentration=
64μg/mL) has been found to be the most potent. MICs were compared with ciprofloxacin (MIC = 6.25–
12.5μg/mL). SAR indicates that the electronegative groups on the benzene nucleus exhibited good
antimicrobial activity than compounds with positive inductive effects. As seen here, compound 28,MIC
of 64μg/mL, in comparison with MIC of compound 24, which was reported to be 256 and 512μg/mL,
against Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli,correspondingly.57

Novel Benzimidazole- Pyrimidine/Pyrazole/Isoxazole Hybrid Derivatives as Antibacterial Agents
13 novelbenzimidazole hybrid with pyrimidine/pyrazole/isoxazole have been developed (Fig.-11) and their
in vitro antibacterial potential was recorded (agar streak dilution method). Amongst all 13 compounds, 34
and 35 were found to be more potent for their antibacterial potential, as shown by their minimum
inhibitory concentrations. The MIC values of compounds 34 and 35 were similar to theCiprofloxacin
against Staphylococcus epidermidis (MIC: 1.95μg/mL), S. aureus (MIC: 7.81μg/mL), and E. coli (MIC:
7.81μg/mL).58

Antibacterial Potential of Novel Pyrazoles and Pyrazolyl Thiazoles Containing Benzimidazole
Derivatives
21 Novel hybrid derivatives were designed and synthesized (Fig.-12) and the antibacterial potential of the
compounds 4, 5, and 7 were estimated in vitro against tested strains. When compared to the typical
antibiotic Chloramphenicol, compound 5d demonstrated outstanding effectiveness against P.
aeruginosa.The activity of (bis and tris derivatives, 37 and 38) was found to be considerably higher than
that of (mono derivative, 36). This could be owing to the substitution of EWGs in bis heterocyclic
compounds (NO , F, Cl, and Br). Derivatives with these (electron withdrawing) groups (37d, 37e, 37f,

Pyrazolyl-BenzimidazoleHybrids as Antibacterial Agents
The antibacterial potential of synthesized compounds (39, 40, and L, Fig.-13) and metal complexes was
recorded against proposed bacterial strains. Findings were matched to those of chloramphenicol at various
concentrations as a standard. When compared to 39 and 40, the 39 complexes were showing extreme
activity whereas; mononuclear coordination complexes are more active than the free ligand (L).60

Antibacterial Activities of New Benzimidazole-Pyrazole-4-Carbonitriles-Methanone Substituted
Derivatives
Antimicrobial potential of new synthesized library of benzimidazole-pyrazole-4-carbonitriles-methanone
substituted derivatives (Fig.-14) has been estimated (diffusion plate method) against selected bacterial
strains. By comparing the MIC values of synthesized compounds with Amoxicillin, compounds (41a) and
compound 44 were shown to be most potent against S. typhimurium. Derivatives (42b) and (43b) was
excellent potency against S. aureus, with an 18 mm inhibition zone.61

Antibacterial Drugs Based on Novel Benzimidazole1, 2, 3 triazole-sulfonamide Hybrids
1, 2, and 3-triazoles are eye-catching linkers with an extensive assortment of applications. In the synthesis
of bioactive and functional molecules, linking two pharmacophores to create innovative bifunctional
medications has grown more practical and vital.62-67Sulfonamides, also known as sulfa medicines, are
among the oldest pharmaceutical agents used as preventive and chemotherapeutic agents against a variety
of medical conditions.68,69 Hybridization of all three moieties in one scaffold could produce derivatives
with synergistic antibacterial effects.A series of novel benzimidazole‑triazole‑sulfonamide hybrids were
synthesized (Fig.-15) and recorded for antibacterial activity against tested bacterial strains. MIC values of
compounds showed that compound 6a was reported to be a potent antibacterial compound (MIC 32-
64μg/mL). Mono derivatives of 1, 2, and 3-triazole (45a–f) displayed a reduction of antibacterial activity
in contrast to bis derivatives (46a–f).70

Antibacterial Activity of New Quinazoline-Benzimidazole Hybrid Derivatives
Quinazoline is a unique moiety with remarkable activities such as antitubercular, antibacterial (Fig.-16)71,
anticonvulsant72, antifungal73, anti-inflammatory74, and anti-tumor.75 4(3H)-quinazolinones have been
identified as an orally accessible antibacterial agent by Bouley et al.76Antibacterial and antimycobacterial
properties of 15 novel quinazoline benzimidazole synthesized hybrids were assessed. Findings indicated
that the synthesized compounds had S. aureus-specific inhibitory action. With a MIC of 4µg/mL,
Derivatives 47b, 47c, 47h, and 47i (Fig.-17) showed similar antibacterial action. Derivatives 47g, 48a,
48b, and 49 showed considerable potency with MICs of 16 to 64µg/mL. The alteration in -COOH group
at the C-4 phenyl (at the para position) by coupling with modified benzimidazoloamines to different
amides as a result, the derivatives 48a, 48b, 48c, and 48d have been created.With MIC (32- 64 µg/mL),
the synthesized compounds displayed mild suppression against S. aureus. At C-4, 2-trifluoromethyl
benzimidazolo-5-amine was substituted for 2-trifluoromethyl benzimidazolo-5-amine, yielding compound
49 and discovered to be inert. Compound 47f, with a MIC of 8µg/mL, was found to have strong inhibitory
efficacy against M. tuberculosis.77

CONCLUSION
Antibiotic resistance is predicted to kill ten million people each year by 2050, in accordance with the
World Health Organization, necessitating the availability of novel medicines. Benzimidazole derivatives
